page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6455B processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, corresponding to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a high clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU includes 60 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This memory configuration yields a strong write bandwidth of 88.2 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2246.0 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 14.94 GB/sec.

No local NVMe storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Network connectivity is delivered at a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-frequency single-core execution combined with dense, high-speed memory, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment.

This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications that also benefit from high CPU clock speeds. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, in-memory caches like Redis, data compilation, and small-to-medium scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR5 RAM running at 5 GHz. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6455B processor at 3.9 GHz, it delivers top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2246.0 and strong memory write bandwidth of 88.2 GB/sec. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The processor includes 60 MB of L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning acceleration, making it highly efficient for specialized computational tasks without requiring dedicated GPU hardware. It is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads requiring high-frequency processing, such as database operations, memory-cached applications, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance.

tagline: High-frequency processing combined with dense DDR5 memory delivers exceptional performance for demanding database operations and single-threaded enterprise workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and bzip3 benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
